what_it_does: Renders a browser page that loads a workshop story JSON tree and DNA runtime data, derives four presentation tiers from that tree, and displays them as a tagline plus one-page, three-page, and eight-page deck strips.
observations: The page is titled "One Story, Four Cuts" and frames the same story tree as multiple tiered materializations.
It fetches `content/stories/workshop.json` and DNA data for `sequence` and `tokens`.
It computes role warmth from `SEQ.narrative_roles` and uses `DNA.surfaces` to select surface colors for each miniature page.
It defines simplified zone glyphs for archetypes including immersive, split-bleed, duo, grid, and stack.
It builds tiers from `DNA.story.cut` for `full`, `teaser`, and `one-pager`, then injects the generated HTML into `#tiers`.
It mounts a story atlas and a Vee dock after rendering setup.
standouts: The file presents narrative count changes as role fusion rather than simple summarization.
Miniature deck pages are generated from structural story cuts and decorated with archetype-specific silhouettes.
The visible page text explicitly instructs readers to read each tier's titles as a complete telling.
conventions: Uses inline page-specific CSS while relying on generated surface variables for color and shadow values.
Uses async inline script for data loading and DOM generation.
Uses template literals to compose HTML strings for repeated page and tier structures.
Keeps the page self-contained apart from runtime, stylesheet, data, and component imports.
concerns: Generated HTML strings interpolate story data directly into markup without escaping.
The script assumes the DNA globals, fetched JSON, role envelopes, and token color paths are all present and valid.
The final `DNA.mountAtlas('story')` call is outside the async data-loading block and may run independently of fetch completion.
notes: A specimen page for showing one story tree as multiple deck resolutions with role-fusion framing.
questions: What exact schema is guaranteed for `content/stories/workshop.json`?
Should story titles and root lines be treated as trusted HTML-safe content?
Is `DNA.mountAtlas('story')` intended to run before or after tier rendering completes?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: the file presents one story at four cuts and renders each cut as a complete tiered specimen.
intention_signals: It seems intended to demonstrate that a single story tree can produce complete deck-like artifacts at multiple page counts while preserving narrative roles through fusion.
novelty: The distinct feature is the combination of story-tree cuts, role warmth, and archetype silhouettes into a compact visual ledger of deck resolutions.
contribution: This file contributes a neutral specimen view that keeps tiered story cuts, role-fusion explanation, surface-token styling, miniature archetype glyphs, and title-chain reading as reusable interpretive material.
summary_for_embedding: An HTML specimen page that fetches a workshop story tree, cuts it into tagline, one-page, teaser, and full tiers, and renders each tier as styled miniature deck artifacts using DNA surface data.
intention_for_embedding: It was made to show how one narrative structure can be viewed as several complete presentation resolutions through role fusion and deck-style visual treatment.
